methods.description: Process for analyzing stable isotopes in sediment and foraminifera samples to determine carbon and nitrogen isotope ratios.

steps.description: Vapour phase digestion was used to remove carbonate from sediment samples.
steps.searchable_summary: remove carbonate from sediment samples using vapour phase digestion

steps.description: Calcareous foraminifera were acidified with 10 % v/v HCl prior to analysis.

Foraminifera from the Pakistan margin were analysed using a Europa Hydra 20 / 20 isotope ratio mass spectrometer (University of California Davis); δ 15 N and δ 13 C of sediments and POM were analysed using VG Prism III isotope ratio mass spectrometer (University of Edinburgh).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotope ratios using mass spectrometers

steps.description: Stable isotope ratios are expressed as (δX)‰ = [R sample / R standard −1] × 1000, where X is either 13 C or 15 N, and R is either ( 13 C : 12 C) or ( 15 N : 14 N), respectively.
Analytical accuracy for Pakistan margin samples was determined using a sucrose standard for δ 13 C and was −23.84 ‰ ± 0.04 ‰ and using an ammonium sulfate standard for δ 15 N at 1.28 ‰ ± 0.19 ‰.
Analytical precision was determined using an internal standard (tissue from the holothurian Oneirophanta mutabilis; Iken et al., 2001), which was sent to each lab.
steps.searchable_summary: determine analytical precision using internal standard

methods.description: Field sampling procedures for collecting sediment, foraminifera, and water samples from the Oman and Pakistan margins in the Arabian Sea.

steps.description: Sampling was carried out during three cruises of the RRS Charles Darwin (CD).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct sampling during RRS Charles Darwin cruises

steps.description: Bottom-water oxygen data were obtained from 4 and 22 CTD casts off Oman and Pakistan, respectively, between bottom-water depths of 140 and 2900 m.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain bottom-water oxygen data from CTD casts

steps.description: Sediments were collected using a hydraulically damped Bowers and Connelly megacorer equipped with eight core tubes (inner diameter of 9.6 cm).
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediments using Bowers and Connelly megacorer

steps.description: The surface layer (0-1 cm) was frozen, freeze-dried and analysed for the following parameters: organic carbon content ( % C org ), total nitrogen (TN %) and stable carbon (δ 13 C) and nitrogen (δ 15 N) ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze surface layer for organic carbon, total nitrogen, and stable isotope ratios

steps.description: During the cruise CD151 at the Pakistan margin two cores per megacorer deployment at each depth were processed for foraminifera (> 300 µm).
Foraminifera were sorted to species level and cleaned in filtered (2 µm screen) seawater to remove any attached organic particles.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and clean foraminifera to species level
